Select a mode above, then Start. Auto Fixed/Smart require a live Excel connection.

🚂 Generate Trailer

Define one or more trailer trains. Axle #1 of each train sits at the entered X position; every following axle = previous axle's X − line spacing, all sharing the same Y position and VCG. New trains start with the same line spacing and X position as the trailer above them. Split each train's axle range into axle groups (e.g. axles 1–5 @ 25 t, axles 6–10 @ 50 t) and pick one of the 4 fixed groups for each range from the dropdown — or mark a group No line load for axles that carry no cargo weight. Every range assigned to the same group (even across different trains) shares that one color, shown below, in the preview, and on the 2D/3D dashboard. Rows are written to Load_Editor!B (name, e.g. Group_1_line_1) and D:G starting at row 124 (D = Line load t, E = X, F = Y, G = VCG). Use "Load from Excel" to pull an already-generated trailer back in for editing. Tick "Show preview beside input data" or "Show preview in separate window" below to keep the live graphic visible while you edit.

📋 Load Editor

Cell-for-cell replica of the Excel Load_Editor sheet — same row numbers, same column letters, same header text. Every cell is editable: type a plain value, or an Excel-style =formula referencing other Load_Editor cells (e.g. =D14*C13/100) or the CREST_301 sheet (e.g. =CREST_301!C4), calculated live in the browser by an embedded HyperFormula engine. Column D from row 14 (Filling[mT]) and columns D:G from row 124 (RO-RO Line load/Xpos/Ypos/VCG) stay specially linked to the dashboard's live tank/axle data (blue cells) instead of the formula engine. Example of formuala.=CREST_301!C4 → Loa, ='CREST_301'!Q30 → tank X_aft geometry =B14*'CREST_301'!C13→ Load_Editor cell × CREST_301 Lightweight Just like Excel: drag a column header's right edge (or a row number's bottom edge) to resize it, and click a cell then drag the small blue square at its bottom-right corner to fill/copy — formulas adjust their cell references automatically (e.g. =B1+10 in A1 becomes =B2+10 when filled into A2), and a $ before a column or row (e.g. $A$1) locks it in place. Every amber cell edit is also saved to Firebase automatically, so it's still here next time you reopen this page — even outside Excel. No internet? Use the "💾 Save file" / "📂 Load file" buttons in the header instead to carry your data between offline PCs (e.g. via a USB drive).

Refreshing…
